children.name: ParseCommand
children.purpose: Extract and validate the command field from the input JSON
children.behavior: Parse the input JSON to extract the 'command' field and validate it is one of the allowed commands (send, history, create_channel, join). Return the command and remaining payload.
postconditions: Command is one of: send, history, create_channel, join
guarantees: Returns error if command is missing or invalid

constraints.description: Command must be one of: send, history, create_channel, join

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid JSON with a valid command, returns command and payload
Given valid JSON with invalid command, returns error

children.name: SendMessage
children.purpose: Send a message to a joined channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the specified channel, then store the message in the messages data store with timestamp and user info.
preconditions: User is a member of the channel
postconditions: Message is stored in messages data store
guarantees: Returns error if user not in channel

inputs.name: payload
inputs.description: Contains channel_id, user_id, and message text

children.signature: def SendMessage(payload: dict, channels: DataStore, messages: DataStore) -> dict

data_operations.description: Read channel membership to validate user
Write new message to messages data store

constraints.description: User must be a member of the channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid payload with user in channel, stores message and returns success
Given user not in channel, returns error
Given empty message text, returns error

children.name: GetHistory
children.purpose: Retrieve message history for a channel
children.behavior: Validate that the user is a member of the channel, then fetch up to 100 most recent messages from the messages data store for that channel.
preconditions: User is a member of the channel
guarantees: Returns error if user not in channel
children.signature: def GetHistory(payload: dict, channels: DataStore, messages: DataStore) -> list

data_operations.description: Read channel membership to validate user
Read messages from messages data store

constraints.description: User must be a member of the channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid payload with user in channel, returns up to 100 messages
Given user not in channel, returns error
Given channel with more than 100 messages, returns only 100 most recent

children.name: CreateChannel
children.behavior: Create a new channel entry in the channels data store with a unique channel_id and add the creator as the first member.
preconditions: Channel name is non-empty and unique
postconditions: Channel is created and creator is a member
guarantees: Returns error if channel name already exists

outputs.name: channel_id
outputs.description: Unique identifier for the new channel

children.signature: def CreateChannel(payload: dict, channels: DataStore) -> str

data_operations.description: Read existing channels to check uniqueness, then write new channel

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid payload with unique channel name, creates channel and returns channel_id
Given empty channel name, returns error
Given duplicate channel name, returns error

children.name: JoinChannel
children.behavior: Validate that the channel exists and the user is not already a member, then add the user to the channel's member list.
preconditions: User is not already a member
postconditions: User is added to channel member list
guarantees: Returns error if channel does not exist or user already member
children.signature: def JoinChannel(payload: dict, channels: DataStore) -> dict

data_operations.description: Read channel membership to validate, then write updated membership

constraints.description: User must not already be a member

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid payload with existing channel and non-member user, adds user and returns success
Given user already member, returns error

children.name: FormatResponse
children.behavior: Take the result from the executed command and format it into a standard JSON response with success, data, and message fields.
postconditions: Output is valid JSON with success, data, message
guarantees: Always returns a consistent response structure

inputs.name: command_result
inputs.description: Result from the executed child function

children.signature: def FormatResponse(command_result: Any, command: str) -> Any

constraints.description: Output must be valid JSON with fields: success, data, message

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a successful command result, returns JSON with success: true and appropriate data
Given an error result, returns JSON with success: false and error message

governance_notes: All children are pure functions or atomic operations that access data stores only as needed. ParseCommand and FormatResponse are pure functions with no data store access. SendMessage, GetHistory, CreateChannel, and JoinChannel are atomic operations that access the channels and/or messages data stores. The parent ChatApp orchestrates the flow: ParseCommand -> route to appropriate handler -> FormatResponse. No child has global variables. Data operations are limited to the specific data stores each child needs.
